commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainer review is required because the PR is maintainer-labeled, merge-conflicting, and changes fail-closed install security plus upgrade behavior.; Needs attention: The hardening direction is security-positive, but the PR makes ClawHub trust endpoint data and official-source bypasses part of the install security boundary.; Review finding: [P2] Remove the unrelated non-ClawHub install warning — src/cli/plu... (sha=34513c69dbafb094530dbec7dcac9b912ef147d4)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds ClawHub release trust checks and acknowledgement plumbing for plugin and skill install/update flows, including CLI, Gateway, Control UI, persisted trust metadata, docs, and tests.

PR surface: Source +2719, Tests +4609, Docs +38, Other +21. Total +7387 across 74 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: the PR diff shows every non-suppressed non-ClawHub plugin install logs the new warning. The central change is security policy hardening rather than a current-main bug repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review is required because the PR is maintainer-labeled, merge-conflicting, and changes fail-closed install security plus upgrade behavior.

target.title: Check ClawHub trust before plugin and skill installs
target.body:
## Summary OpenClaw now checks ClawHub trust before installing or updating community plugins and skills, and it stops malicious or ClawHub-blocked releases before download. Suspicious community releases require explicit acknowledgement, while official ClawHub sources and bundled OpenClaw sources skip the trust/security/policy friction entirely. This applies across CLI install/update, chat/control skill and plugin flows, doctor/onboarding repair paths, and gateway skill-install responses. The official-skill path consumes ClawHub publisher metadata (`owner.official: true`), so official publishers such as NVIDIA, Peter Steinberger, and OpenClaw are treated as trusted once ClawHub exposes that field on skill metadata. ## What Changed - Community ClawHub plugins and skills: install/update now reads ClawHub security and moderation state before download; malicious or quarantined releases block, and suspicious releases require acknowledgement. - Plugin and skill updates: the latest release is checked before it replaces the installed version; blocked latest releases leave the existing install unchanged and recommend uninstall when the installed release is now malicious. - Official ClawHub packages: default ClawHub packages marked `channel: "official"` / `isOfficial: true` bypass ClawHub trust prompts, install-policy hooks, denylist scans, and before-install hooks. - Official ClawHub skill publishers: default ClawHub skill detail with `owner.official: true` bypasses trust prompts and policy friction. The regression fixture uses NVIDIA's `tao-setup-nvidia-gpu-host` shape to prove this path. - Bundled OpenClaw sources: bundled plugins such as `openclaw/kitchen-sink` and bundled skills such as `peekaboo` bypass the same install friction because they are first-party product code, not community downloads. - Chat/control UI surfaces: install trust failures are propagated through gateway protocol details so chat and control flows can present the same blocked/suspicious decision instead of hiding it behind a generic failure. ## Behavior Matrix | Source / release state | Install behavior | Update behavior | Extra policy friction | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Community malicious or quarantined plugin/skill | Blocks before download. | Blocks latest release before replacing the installed version. | Not applicable; ClawHub block wins. | | Community suspicious plugin/skill | Requires interactive acknowledgement or `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk`. | Same acknowledgement gate before update. | Normal policy and hook checks still run after acknowledgement. | | Community clean plugin/skill | Installs normally. | Updates normally. | Normal policy and hook checks still run. | | Official ClawHub plugin | Installs normally without trust prompt even if scanner metadata is noisy. | Updates normally without trust prompt. | Bypasses install-policy hooks, denylist scans, and before-install hooks. | | Official ClawHub skill publisher | Installs normally when skill metadata includes `owner.official: true`. | Same trusted-source behavior for resolver-backed updates. | Bypasses install-policy hooks, denylist scans, and before-install hooks. | | Bundled OpenClaw plugin/skill | Installs/loads as first-party OpenClaw code. | Managed by the product/bundled source path. | Bypasses install-policy hooks, denylist scans, and before-install hooks. | ## Proof Behavioral proof: - Community malicious plugin and skill fixtures return blocked ClawHub trust metadata; install exits non-zero before archive download, and update refuses to replace the installed version. - Community suspicious plugin and skill fixtures require acknowledgement; without acknowledgement the command exits non-zero, and with `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk` the same release installs or updates. - Official ClawHub plugin fixture uses `channel: "official"` / `isOfficial: true`; install succeeds with no ClawHub warning even though it uses the same trust-check entry point. - Official ClawHub skill fixture uses `owner: { handle: "nvidia", displayName: "NVIDIA", official: true }` for `tao-setup-nvidia-gpu-host`; the test asserts ClawHub security verdicts are not fetched and the install source is recorded as `{ kind: "clawhub", authority: "official", mutable: false, network: true }`. - Bundled OpenClaw plugin fixture uses `openclaw/kitchen-sink`; bundled skill fixture uses `peekaboo`; both assert install-policy, dependency-denylist, and before-install hook surfaces are not called. - Live ClawHub package metadata sampled for `@openclaw/matrix` returns `channel: "official"`, `isOfficial: true`, and a noisy scan state; the OpenClaw official-plugin path treats that as trusted and reaches only normal compatibility checks on this stale PR base. Current ClawHub API caveat: - Production skill detail responses sampled for Peter/NVIDIA identify the owner but do not yet expose `owner.official`. This PR consumes the official publisher field correctly; ClawHub still needs to expose that field on v1 skill detail/search responses for live official skill publishers to bypass trust friction in production. ## Verification Reviewer-checkable behavior: - `src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts` includes the NVIDIA-owned `tao-setup-nvidia-gpu-host` official-publisher fixture and asserts no ClawHub security verdict lookup happens. - `src/plugins/install-security-scan.runtime.test.ts` includes the `openclaw/kitchen-sink` bundled plugin fixture and the `peekaboo` bundled skill fixture, and asserts install-policy, dependency-denylist, and hook runners are skipped. - `.artifacts/clawhub-install-trust/run-proof.cjs` was rerun at PR head `34513c69dbafb094530dbec7dcac9b912ef147d4` and regenerated the local terminal transcript for every plugin/skill install and update case. Automated checks: - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.install.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/install-security-scan.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts` passed: 438 tests across 3 shards. - `git diff --check` passed. - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` passed. - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo` passed. Not run: - Full `pnpm check`, full build, and Crabbox/Testbox were not rerun in this checkout. - Latest live official ClawHub plugin packages require newer OpenClaw compatibility than this stale PR base (`2026.6.8`). A branch refresh onto current `origin/main` (`2026.6.10`) should allow the same live official plugin installs to pass compatibility after the trust-bypass path. ## Screenshots These GitHub-hosted terminal screenshots are visual proof for the CLI prompt and block/acknowledgement states. The proof generator was rerun locally at current PR head; the uploaded images below are kept as reviewer-visible visual references for the same scenario set. | Screenshot group | Claim proved | Crop choice | | --- | --- | --- | | Plugin installs | Malicious blocks, suspicious acknowledgement gate, clean community install, and official/source-linked plugin install. | Element crop of terminal panel. | | Skill installs | Same trust gate for archive-backed skills, including owner-qualified labels and official/source-linked skill metadata. | Element crop of terminal panel. | | Plugin updates | Malicious latest release blocks download, suspicious latest release requires acknowledgement, and installed plugin remains unchanged when blocked. | Element crop of terminal panel. | | Skill updates | Same malicious/suspicious update behavior for archive-backed skills. | Element crop of terminal panel. |
Plugin install screenshots ### Plugin malicious release blocked before download ### Plugin suspicious release blocked without acknowledgement ### Plugin suspicious release succeeds with acknowledgement ### Plugin community clean install succeeds ### Plugin official/source-linked clean install succeeds
Skill install screenshots ### Skill malicious release blocked before download ### Skill suspicious release blocked without acknowledgement ### Skill suspicious release succeeds with acknowledgement ### Skill community clean install succeeds ### Skill official/source-linked clean install succeeds
Plugin update screenshots ### Plugin update blocks malicious latest release ### Plugin update blocks suspicious latest release without acknowledgement ### Plugin update allows suspicious latest release with acknowledgement
Skill update screenshots ### Skill update blocks malicious latest release ### Skill update blocks suspicious latest release without acknowledgement ### Skill update allows suspicious latest release with acknowledgement
target.author: jesse-merhi
